content:
{
    "improvements": [
        {
            "id": "IMP-001",
            "description": "Define the cross-process caching mechanism for probe results",
            "rationale": "Bash, Python, and TS components all need access to the 60s cached probe results (AC-1.2). Without a shared mechanism (e.g., tmpfs file, Redis, or shared memory), each runtime will probe independently, violating the <500ms overhead constraint (NFR-Perf-1).",
            "location": "Section 4.1 (AC-1.2) & Section 5.1 (NFR-Perf-2)",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.95
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-002",
            "description": "Add cycle detection to the fallback resolver",
            "rationale": "Misconfiguration in `model-config.yaml` or `model_aliases_extra` could create infinite fallback loops (e.g., Model A -> Model B -> Model A). The resolver must detect and break cycles to prevent stack overflows or infinite hangs during resolution.",
            "location": "Section 4.3 (AC-3.2)",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.95
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-003",
            "description": "Define active alerting mechanism for smoke-fleet vendor regressions",
            "rationale": "AC-5.3 logs to JSONL and NOTES.md, but M5 requires <24h operator surfacing. Passive logs won't meet this SLA if the operator isn't actively checking the repo. Needs a push notification (e.g., webhook, automated issue creation).",
            "location": "Section 4.5 (AC-5.3 & AC-5.4)",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.9
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-004",
            "description": "Implement cross-reference validation for fallback chains",
            "rationale": "JSON Schema (AC-2.4) can enforce array length (>=2), but cannot verify that the `model_id` strings actually exist elsewhere in the registry. A semantic validation step is needed at load time to prevent runtime ROUTING_MISS errors.",
            "location": "Section 4.2 (AC-2.4)",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-005",
            "description": "Specify file locking for smoke-fleet and audit log appends",
            "rationale": "Multiple parallel CI jobs or multi-model runs writing to `smoke-fleet/{date}.jsonl` or audit logs simultaneously will cause race conditions and corrupted JSONL files.",
            "location": "Section 4.5 (AC-5.3) & Section 5.3",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-006",
            "description": "Differentiate between local network failure and provider API failure",
            "rationale": "If the runner loses internet access, all probes will fail, triggering a total probe failure (L10) and false alarms. The probe gate should verify basic connectivity (e.g., pinging a reliable external IP) before marking all providers as DEGRADED.",
            "location": "Section 4.1 (AC-1.2) & Section 7.1 (R4)",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-007",
            "description": "Define credential injection strategy for multi-language probes",
            "rationale": "The PRD requires pinging providers (AC-1.2) across Bash, Python, and TS. It must explicitly state how API keys are securely distributed to these disparate runtimes to perform the probes without leaking into logs or requiring duplicate configuration.",
            "location": "Section 5.2 (Security)",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-008",
            "description": "Implement a maximum cost cap for fallback chains",
            "rationale": "Falling back from a cheap primary model to an expensive fallback model (e.g., Haiku to Opus) could rapidly exhaust budgets. The `cost_check_budget` (L2) needs to account for the worst-case cost of the fallback chain, or allow operators to cap fallback costs.",
            "location": "Section 4.3 (AC-3.2) & Section 5.6 (L2)",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.75
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-009",
            "description": "Add a data retention policy for smoke-fleet JSONL files",
            "rationale": "Appending to `grimoires/loa/a2a/smoke-fleet/{date}.jsonl` weekly will accumulate indefinitely. A log rotation or cleanup policy is needed to prevent repository bloat over time.",
            "location": "Section 4.5 (AC-5.3)",
            "priority": "LOW",
            "confidence": 0.7
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-010",
            "description": "Clarify the runtime evaluation of the LOA_FORCE_LEGACY_MODELS kill switch",
            "rationale": "It is unclear if changing this environment variable requires a full system restart, a rebuild of the TS codegen (AC-4.3), or if it is evaluated dynamically per-request. This impacts incident response times.",
            "location": "Section 4.3 (AC-3.4) & Section 5.4 (NFR-BC-2)",
            "priority": "LOW",
            "confidence": 0.75
        }
    ],
    "summary": "10 improvements identified,
